resources.string: The path begins where the map ends
Continue — locked until you register and start a new game
Please register first — then Continue will unlock!

To create an account, save progress, and sync your adventure, we process your email and optional profile data. Data is stored on secure Firebase servers. You can request deletion of your account and data by contacting support.\n\nFull privacy policy is available on our website. By continuing, you confirm that you have read this notice and agree to the processing of personal data under applicable law (including GDPR where applicable).
I agree to the processing of my personal data
Continue your saga on this device
A quick registration before your journey begins
We will send a reset link to your email
Check your inbox for the reset link
Already have an account? Sign in
Password must be at least 8 characters
No account found for this email
Something went wrong. Please try again
An old miller died, leaving his three sons a modest inheritance: a mill, a donkey, and a cat.
My brothers took the mill and the donkey, and I only got this cat. What use is such an inheritance?
Don\'t be sad, master! Give me a bag and a pair of boots, and I\'ll make you rich. You\'ll see that you got the best share!
The younger son was surprised to hear the cat speak, but decided to trust him. And so began an amazing adventure...
Thank you for completing the game!

Sunlight filters through the dusty slats of the old mill, playing across sacks of flour and creaking shelves. The air smells of warmth, morning freshness, and a subtle unease - as if the world is preparing you for something significant. You are the Puss in Boots. Today promises changes that cannot be ignored.\n\nOn the miller\'s table lies a letter, its seal gleaming with golden wax. The wind gently stirs the edge of the parchment, almost nudging you to act.
A letter... I wonder what\'s inside? It doesn\'t seem like ordinary paper. I can smell the road and... adventure. Maybe it\'s time to act?

You carefully pick up your boots. The leather rustles softly, the fur on your paws lifts slightly. The world beyond the mill looks inviting, mysterious.\n\nYou realize - it\'s time to decide. What will you do?
I\'ll take matters into my own paws and go first!
I need to learn more first, observe the road and the plan.
Hmm... what if I play a little trick and see what happens?
You decide which path to take. Each choice shapes your cunning, bravery, and creativity.
Now you must decide what to do with the letter. It smells of distant lands and a castle where an inheritance awaits. Each option opens a new path.
I\'ll hide the letter and keep it to myself. No one should know until it\'s safe.
I\'ll show it to the miller. Planning together is better than risking it alone.
I\'ll use the letter as bait - curious to see who shows interest and how they react.
Depending on your choice, the world reacts differently. A hidden letter - stealth and caution; a shared discussion - trust and cooperation; playing with the letter - cunning and observation.
You grab your bag. Boots tightly fastened, fur on your paws bristling with excitement. How will you prepare for the journey?
Only the essentials - agility matters more than load.
Better take extra supplies - you never know what lies ahead.
I\'ll grab a few odd things that seem useless - just in case they\'re needed.
You pack your things, feeling the thrill and weight. The world beckons beyond the mill. With each step toward the door, each breath becomes part of your future story.
You peek out the window. The wind stirs the grass, the road winding through the forested hills. Bridges, rivers, forests, and trials await. The first step is nearly taken.
I\'ll step onto the road immediately - forward, without looking back!
First, I\'ll look around, study the route and possible obstacles.
I\'ll play a small trick - leave markings and check who\'s nearby.
You make your choice. The mill fades behind you, the road calls. Each step, each bit of cunning and decision begins to shape you - the Puss in Boots - the hero-to-be.
